text:
There was a curious little blue bird perched atop a pine tree. Every day, after the sun had waned and the stars began to blink their gentle light from the dark sky, this tiny bird would swoop down into a small creek to sip its sweet water.

There was a wise old owl who lived deep in the woods nearby. Each dawn, he'd see the little blue bird flutter down into the creek. Intrigued, the owl would glide silently until he reached a branch just within reach.

"Why do you never leave there?" the owl would ask one morning.

The blue bird would chirp softly. "I love that spot. I always come back here after a bath."

The wise owl pondered this. Then he proposed a bet. "If you ever leave that spot for something else, I'll sing loudly enough to scare you away."

With a cheeky little flick of its wings, the blue bird buzzed down,landing right beside the owl.

"Weisslei,"
